title: Bill Cosby found guilty of sexual assault, ordered to pay nearly $60 million in damages
content: Jurors found the former television star liable for the sexual battery and assault of Donna Motsinger. They awarded her $17.5 million in past damages and $1.75 million for future damages. In a second phase of the trial Monday afternoon, they awarded an additional $40 million in punitive damages.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Army plane crash in Colombia kills at least 66 soldiers and crew
content: The Lockheed C-130 Hercules crashed shortly after takeoff from Puerto Leguizamo in southern Colombia.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as new chief of Department of Homeland Security
content: Mullin, a Trump ally, replaces Kristi Noem, who was fired earlier this month for her role in the large-scale operation against undocumented migrants in Minnesota.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: In Italy, Giorgia Meloni's lost referendum opens a new chapter in her term
content: The judicial reform project submitted to voters by the Italian prime minister was rejected by 53.7% of the vote, after a tense campaign and amid high voter turnout.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: EU and Australia strike free trade deal after years of talks
content: Ursula von der Leyen and Anthony Albanese announced a compromise in Canberra aimed at boosting exports and strengthening economic ties amid global trade uncertainty.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: TotalEnergies bows to Trump administration, dropping two offshore wind projects in favor of oil and gas investments
content: The French company is ending two offshore wind turbine programs off the coasts of New York and North Carolina, totaling 4 gigawatts of power. It will receive a $928 million reimbursement on the condition that the funds are reinvested in hydrocarbon projects.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Mette Frederiksen heads into Danish elections as a global figure, but a divisive one domestically
content: The leader of the Social Democrats, in power since 2019, seeks reelection on Tuesday. While her support for Ukraine and her firm stance against Donald Trump regarding Greenland have earned her recognition on the European stage, her party has been struggling in the polls.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Marine Le Pen voices support for her 'friend' Viktor Orban
content: The nationalist prime minister is taking advantage of opportunities to rally his foreign supporters, particularly Europeans, in Budapest ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, which could prove difficult for him.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: EU says reports that Hungary leaked information to Russia are 'greatly concerning'
content: European security officials reportedly said that the Hungarian foreign minister called his Russian counterpart during breaks in EU meetings to give 'direct reports on what was discussed.'
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: EU speeds up free trade with Mercosur treaty and new Australia agreement
content: South America, India and now Australia. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen welcomed on Monday the 'trade trilogy' that Brussels has built in just a few months to boost the EU's exchanges with the rest of the world despite strong resistance, particularly from the agricultural sector.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Strategic battle unfolds between Israel and Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
content: Khiam is the last Shiite-majority town on the road leading to the northeast of Lebanon. For its residents, already scarred by several wars, the ongoing battle has reopened internal divisions and the traumas of the Israeli occupation. Tens of thousands of residents have found themselves trapped.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Russia-linked tanker seized by France arrives in Marseille
content: The Deyna, a 250-meter-long vessel that set sail from Murmansk in Russia under a Mozambican flag, is under EU sanctions and is the third such tanker intercepted by France in recent months.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Italian PM Meloni admits referendum defeat, but vows to stay on
content: Voters rejected a judicial reform backed by the far-right prime minister, delivering a setback to the right-wing government one year ahead of national elections.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: US and TotalEnergies reach 'nearly $1 billion' deal to end offshore wind projects
content: The US and the French company signed an agreement to end TotalEnergies' offshore US wind farm projects and redirect those funds towards fossil fuel production.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May 1
content: The mammoth deal to eliminate tariffs on more than 90% of trade between the two blocs has proven divisive in Europe, with France leading opposition over concerns some of its farmers will be worse off because of it.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to apply provisionally from May 1
content: The pact still needs a green light from lawmakers in the European Parliament, which referred it to the EU's top court. But the European Commission has opted for a provisional application.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Greece: Tempi train disaster trial postponed amid courtroom chaos
content: The families of the 57 people killed in the head-on collision of two trains on February 28, 2023, voiced their anger in court, denouncing the venue as unsuitable for the trial. Thirty-six people face charges over the deadly accident.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Trump says he had 'very good' talks with Iran, while Tehran denies they happened
content: The announcement came ahead of a deadline imposed by the US president to reopen the Strait of Hormuz shipping lane. Tehran accused the US president of manipulating energy markets, and Israel said it would keep up strikes on Lebanon and the Islamic Republic.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: Electricity prices: 'The Iberian solution has significantly reduced consumer bills without public spending'
content: Because soaring electricity prices across Europe stem from a flaw in the market, with gas still setting the price for all, the European Union should address the root cause and take inspiration from the mechanism in Spain and Portugal, economist Natalia Fabra argues.
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.

title: UK police investigate arson attack on four Jewish organization ambulances in London
content: 'The arson attack is being treated as an antisemitic hate crime,' said London's Metropolitan Police, adding that they were 'looking for three suspects.' Prime Minister Keir Starmer said 'antisemitism has no place in our society.'
sourceDescription: World - Discover all the articles, videos and infographics in the World section on Le Monde.fr.
